title: Build SkillService with loc.en resolution and provenance
description:
Rewrite SkillService to consume the GameTora snapshot format instead of the current master.mdb-extracted skills.json. Must:

1. Resolve loc.en overrides at load time — when loc.en exists for a skill, use its condition_groups/effects/char instead of JP top-level
2. Expose provenance via release_en field — isReleased(skillId) method
3. Expose activationCheck (guaranteed | wit-check) from the activation field
4. Preserve existing query methods (getById, getAll, getByGroupId, etc.)
5. Integrate with existing simulatability layer (isSimulatable)
6. Derive skill group relationships at runtime from groupId

Depends on the GameTora snapshot pipeline being in place.
close_reason: Implemented: GameTora skill loader with loc.en resolution, merged 2049 skills (565 released + 1484 upcoming), isReleased/getActivationCheck APIs. All tests passing.

title: Build GameTora snapshot fetch pipeline
description:
Create the core sync infrastructure that fetches GameTora's manifest, compares content hashes against local data-manifest.json, and re-fetches only changed keys. This replaces the entity catalog role currently handled by master.mdb extraction.

Must fetch and store: skills, character-cards, support-cards, training events, and dict data. Store as-is in GameTora's flat format with loc.en overrides intact. Write data-manifest.json tracking synced hashes.

Implement as 'bun run sync:data' command.

See ADR-0001 for the design rationale.
close_reason: Implemented sync:data command with incremental hash-based fetching, shared gametora-client.ts, 12 snapshot files committed.

title: Build UmaService and SupportCardService for GameTora snapshot
description:
Create/rewrite UmaService and SupportCardService to consume GameTora snapshot format (character-cards, support-cards). Must:

1. Resolve loc.en overrides at load time
2. Expose provenance via release_en — isReleased(id) method
3. Support cards: merge training event data (eventSkills from all 5 categories)
4. Uma outfits: expose skills_unique, skills_innate, skills_awakening, aptitudes, stats
5. Preserve existing query methods used by UI consumers

Depends on the GameTora snapshot pipeline being in place.
close_reason: Implemented: GameTora loaders for umas (129) and support cards (536, 208 released), SupportCardService with full query API, UmaService.isReleased, dev page updated.

title: Add skill simulatability validation layer
description:
Skills from upcoming/future content may contain condition tokens or effect types the current simulation engine doesn't recognize. The condition parser crashes at runtime on unknown tokens (undefined lookup) rather than failing gracefully.

Need a validation layer that:
1. Checks each skill's condition/precondition strings against the parser's known condition tokens
2. Tags skills that fail validation as non-simulatable
3. Guards buildSkillData so a single unparseable skill doesn't crash the entire sim run
4. Makes the simulatable/non-simulatable distinction available to UI consumers

This unblocks safely displaying upcoming content while preventing sim crashes.
design:
Approach:
- Export known condition token set from defaultConditions (Object.keys)
- Add extractConditionTokens() utility that tokenizes a condition string
- Add isSimulatable(skill) to SkillService — validates alternatives' conditions against known tokens
- Add try-catch guard in buildSkillData as safety net — log warning, return empty triggers on parse failure
- Simulatable flag derived at runtime from condition map, not stored in JSON

Display vs Simulation boundary:
- Display features (charts, search, filtering): show ALL skills, badge non-simulatable
- Simulation features (compare mode, skill planner): silently exclude non-simulatable
close_reason: Implemented: knownConditionTokens set, extractConditionTokens tokenizer, isSimulatable/filterSimulatable/getUnsupportedTokens on SkillService, safety guard in buildSkillData. 17 tests passing.

title: Extract activationCheck from master.mdb skill_data
description:
The activate_lot column in skill_data indicates whether a skill requires a wit check (1) or is guaranteed (0). This field should be included in the skill data under the name activationCheck with values 'guaranteed' | 'wit-check'.

Once the GameTora snapshot pipeline is in place, verify GameTora's activation field matches and use that as the source instead. This is a short-term extraction addition if needed before the full migration.
close_reason: Resolved by UMA-kha: GameTora activation field is now exposed as getActivationCheck() on SkillService. No separate master.mdb extraction needed.

title: Slim down master.mdb extraction to course data only
description:
With entity catalog data moving to GameTora snapshots, master.mdb extraction should only handle course geometry (extract-course-data.ts). 

1. Remove extract-skills.ts, extract-support-cards.ts, extract-uma-info.ts from the pipeline
2. Keep extract-course-data.ts as the sole master.mdb consumer
3. Update extract-all.ts to only run course extraction
4. Integrate master.mdb resource version tracking into data-manifest.json
5. Update documentation to reflect the new pipeline

Depends on GameTora snapshot pipeline and new services being in place and validated.

value: 101 skills have different condition_groups between JP (top-level) and Global (loc.en). ALL skills that have a loc.en override have differing conditions — there are zero cases where loc.en exists but conditions match JP. This means loc.en resolution is not optional for simulation correctness on Global.

Skill group structural invariant: within a family (shared groupId), conditions/preconditions/baseDuration/cooldownTime are ALWAYS identical across members (verified: 156 multi-member groups, 0 exceptions). Only effect modifiers and sometimes effect count differ. Multi-alternative skills (2+ condition slots) are exclusively single-member families (unique skills with branching activation, 18 total). These two axes never cross in current data.
